reviews.review_text:
Reviewing (mostly) every game (or DLC) in my library, part 625:

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️☆☆☆☆ (6/10)

Ngl, I'm rather disappointed by [i]Assassin's Creed Unity ("Unity").[/i] I'm pretty active on the [i]Assassin's Creed[/i] subreddit, and everyone has been praising it as an overhated, underrated stealth and parkour masterpiece. And honestly, [i]Unity[/i] was just ... perfectly mid. Don't get me wrong, the game constantly introduces really smart ideas—denser stealth systems, social infiltration, “black box” assassination missions with multiple pathways, murder mystery side quests, and flashy parkour built around momentum and flow—but almost every mechanic feels like it needed another year in the oven. When [i]Unity[/i] works, it feels incredible. When it doesn’t, it feels like the game is actively fighting you. Aggressively mid.

[b][h2]Pros:[/h2][/b]                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
✅ [b]Paris is gorgeous.[/b] Even all these years later, [i]Unity[/i] easily has one of the best cities Ubisoft has ever created. Revolutionary Paris feels dense, crowded, alive, and visually stunning. Massive crowds fill the streets, smoke rises from riots and executions, churches tower over cramped alleyways, and interiors seamlessly blend into the world without loading screens. 

✅ [b]The stealth has some genuinely fantastic ideas.[/b] [i]Unity[/i] tries to push the series closer toward actual stealth rather than simple counter-kill combat, and honestly? I kinda respect it. You have to pay attention to sightlines, guard placement, escape routes, and timing in ways older AC games rarely demanded. The assassination missions themselves are also excellent. The “black box” structure gives you multiple approaches, disguises, entrances, and opportunities to eliminate targets, almost like a mini [i]Hitman[/i] level. I genuinely do not understand why Ubisoft abandoned these mission designs in later RPG entries because they would fit perfectly there.

✅ [b]Murder mystery side content.[/b] Weirdly enough, some of the side content ended up more engaging than the main story itself. The murder mysteries were a really fun change of pace and added variety beyond climbing towers and stabbing guards. Solving crimes by investigating scenes, questioning witnesses, and piecing together clues remided me a lot of [i]Syndicate![/i]

✅ [b]Excellent Steam Deck performance.[/b] Considering [i]Unity’s [/i]infamous technical reputation at launch, I was genuinely shocked by how well it runs on the Steam Deck. The game booted right up with no tweaking required and maintained surprisingly stable performance while moving through the game’s massive crowds. 

[b][h2]Mixed:[/h2][/b]
🟨 [b]Parkour is simultaneously amazing and terrible.[/b] This is probably the best-looking parkour system in the franchise and one of the most infuriating to actually control. Watching Arno descend buildings smoothly, vault across rooftops, or weave through windows looks incredible in motion. The animations are absurdly fluid. Unfortunately, the system also feels wildly inconsistent. Sometimes Arno gracefully flows across the environment like an acrobatic genius; other times he refuses to jump off a wall while guards are shooting at him, launches himself toward the completely wrong object, or clings to random scenery for no reason whatsoever. I could not control him tbh. 

🟨 [b]Combat is clunky but more grounded.[/b] [i]Unity[/i] deserves credit for trying to move away from the effortless “kill twenty guards at once” combat of earlier games. Enemies are more dangerous, parries matter more, and getting surrounded can genuinely kill you. Gunmen are especially terrifying because they can absolutely blast you from off-screen while you’re busy fighting someone else. At the same time, though, the combat often feels awkward and unresponsive rather than intentionally difficult, especially when paired with inconsistent animations and enemy behavior.

[b][h2]Cons:[/h2][/b]
🟥[b] The story and characters are painfully bland.[/b] Arno might genuinely be one of the least interesting protagonists in the franchise. The game clearly wants another Ezio-style revenge story, but Arno never develops enough personality, ideology, or emotional depth to carry it. Instead of feeling invested in the French Revolution or the Assassin Brotherhood, he mostly feels like “some guy” chasing personal revenge while occasionally stumbling into major historical events. The supporting cast does not help much either. Most characters are forgettable, underdeveloped, or unlikable, and the romance feels completely half-baked. The game expects you to care because it tells you to care, rather than actually building convincing chemistry or emotional attachment.

🟥 [b]The game constantly sabotages its own mechanics.[/b] This is the ultimate [i]Unity[/i] problem. Every genuinely smart design choice is paired with technical nonsense that drags it down. Guards can spot you through walls, detect you from absurd distances, or instantly alert half the map. Enemies sometimes shoot through cover. Parkour regularly misreads inputs. Stealth tools work brilliantly one moment and collapse the next because the AI decided to behave unpredictably. 

🟥 [b]The map UI is complete visual overload.[/b] I genuinely do not understand who approved this map design. The second you open it, Paris looks less like a city and more like Ubisoft dumped an entire office supply store of icons onto your screen. Chests, side missions, collectibles, social events, co-op markers, shops, murder mysteries, viewpoints, cafes—there is so much clutter that I couldn't even find the main mission marker. Trying to navigate becomes exhausting because important objectives constantly drown beneath dozens of meaningless distractions. Open-world games already struggle with checklist fatigue, but Unity’s map takes it to absurd levels. Instead of making Paris feel exciting to explore, the UI often makes the city feel like homework.

[hr]
If you enjoyed this review, please check out my curator page to find more: [url=https://store.steampowered.com/curator/45814768-Verdict-Play-or-Pass/]Verdict: Play or Pass. [/url] Also, please leave a like and visit [url=https://steamcommunity.com/groups/Indie_Gems]IndieGems[/url] and our [url=https://www.youtube.com/@IndieGems2]YouTube channel[/url] for more reviews like this one.
[/hr]

the game itself is awesome. The feeling of immersion I get from playing it is literally unmatched. Just walking around, I get the feeling that the NPC's are all real people with lives and things. You'll be running around and see people protesting at Notre Dame, people running away carrying items they looted, etc. The city looks both absolutely stunning and on the brink of chaos all at the same time. The free-running aspect has been much improved, but there are still instances where the game won't fully understand where I'm trying to point Arno and instead run him up a random lightpost mid chase. Assassination missions are completely open to however you want to go about it. You get heavily rewarded for utilizing the stealth aspect of the game, and not running into a situation guns blazing and sword swinging. I rate the game itself a 8/10.

I really don’t know why people hate this one. It was good when it released and it’s good now on my second playthrough. 

I will say, the PC version is a lot less stable than the Xbox version and I’ve had a few issues myself like the desync problems (the irony) when hitting enemies and the free running being a bit glitchy too. FPS drops and no way to fix it as this game doesn’t have DLSS or anything similar. The stuttering sometimes is a big one because it affects cutscenes too not just gameplay. It’s a shame because this was the very end of the good era of Assassins Creed games along with Syndicate before that horrible RPG fantasy switch over we got and the terrible fighting mechanics.

Im going to recommend this game as if the issues can be sorted or you can fight through them then you’ll have a good time experiencing Arno’s journey and won’t regret playing.

Due to frustrating Ubisoft Connect 'security' protocols my account has been suspended for virtually no reason. I am not recommending getting any legacy game from Ubisoft because of this type of bs. Unfortunately even bought on steam it still needs Ubisoft connection. I paid for a game (and many others) which  I can't play. Ubisoft isn't sharing for how long my account has been suspended only that it's due to too many wrong password attempts. And I misspelled it ONCE before they suspended my account without any user friendly way to access it. Their support team is slow and not helpfull.  This is questionable practice and I will submit my complaint to EU regulatory bodies.

It was good, but not great. I feel like back then the gameplay loop was already getting old, and now it's actually not better. Back in Assassins Creed II times I was mesmerized by the Parkour system in the series, but honestly now it feels mostly annoying. It's impossible to play stealth with the character that goes where it wants. The cover to cover gameplay also barely works, it's super hard to get out of cover and back to cover you always get stuck somewhere or start jumping on something. Some enemies are entirely blind, and others can spot you even if you are almost directly above them.

I like the addition of going through interiors (last AC I played was AC: III). Although if they weren't on a pre-designed path it was hard to actually get inside one. At the beginning of the game it felt annoying that I had to find that one open window to each building, even though there is so many that could potentially be open. And at the end, it felt like a dumb repetitive task - the first step to every infiltration mission. I did like the "fortress" missions. Minus all the annoying systemics I've already mentioned. It's just a shame that it feels like there's only 5 types of missions that keep rotating all the time. It felt like towards the end designers were trying to incorporate some other types, showing that we're working together with the companion NPC, but they clearly didn't have the tech for it and they sometimes feel so, so stupid.

And the story doesn't help making them interesting, because the main character was annoying me through the whole game and the story itself is obviously super simple and without a lot of thought. Sometimes I wish that the main characters would think for a second instead of killing everybody who's name they've heard. It's also crazy how little the plot has to do with the French Revolution. Sure, we get told that some people are robbing this palace and we get in touch with historical figures. But I didn't even know when the French Revolution has started in the game story. At some point there were just people on the streets. Meeting Bonaparte was honestly, super cheesy and fake. I wish they would focus more on Arno interacting with the people of France, taking part in these events, instead of going after some girl. The king execution moment was so cool. I wish there were more of them.

I know I'm being harsh and that the game is more than 10 years old at this point. I had good moments - when the parkour was working as I wanted it to, and the weapons I had were at the right level to mission difficulty, then the game can make you feel like an Assassin. And of course, it was also just a very easy play that passed the time very quickly. Parkour isn't great during stealth missions, but it's great for just jumping through the city when you want to go from A to B. But hearing that it's "the best Assassins Creed up to date after the bugs were fixed" I simply had higher expectations. I honestly felt like nowadays a game like this would be considered AA, not AAA. Unless having bazillion collectables is what makes it AAA. It's crazy that after playing through the whole main story my completion rate is lower than 20%, because I didn't always hit all the sub-objectives. The fake replayability value is insane(ly dumb).

I waited to buy this game and play it because I heard about all the issues shortly after launch with crashes and running sluggish. And while I haven't had any crashes, the game is terribly optimized and runs as smooth as sandpaper. Running around or in a fight you get a little lag and suddenly I'm looking up at the sky or in the opposite direction. Just unplayable.

this is a ubisoft complaint. i downloaded the game to play after a very long time. i wanted to start a play through. cant start a new game, cant delete save and start, its connected to the cloud, cant turn off the cloud because app doesnt download and website very slow, so i made a new account so i can login from that play, guess what u can only login in from one account at a time and its locked, deleted the game. 

all this stupidity just to play an offline game.
hate ubisoft i purchases this game long time and now asking me for th account which they suspended it... if you are new to this games i suggest to do crack or fix first you account..

beat the game twice on ps4 and deciding to do it a third time on pc. i've experienced only a couple bugs, some were audio issues, visual issues and even one where i had to restart the game but ultimately none of them take away from the experience. one of my personal favorite games oat even if some ppl do say the story is mid, the gameplay more than makes up for it personally. 

however i do also think the story is nice, there's just a couple things that they missed out on implementing is all

Cant play the game becuase of the stupid ubisoft connect having it binded on the old not new acount that uses a hotmail that was hacked 20 + years ago that microsoft does not help me with recovering. ubisoft connect is a virus.

The first AC game I almost chose to NOT recommend it. I always heard about its problems, I was warned for many years but I didn't know how bad it was until I played it.

Altough I played it fully and fast, the glitches, bugs and crashes are just very stressful upseting. Specially because usually happen after you killed every enemy you could and are about to complete the mission.

I absolutely love the French Revolution set. It's exciting, the world feels alive and there's so much to keep us entertained. But its problems bring shame to a very compelling series. 

Arno is a fun character, but horrible to control. The story is exciting, sometimes reminding me of Black Flag levels of excitement. 

The ledges!!! I'm going to have nightmares about trying to walk on ledges in this game!!

Try it at your own risk.

I know that the game has been out for more than 10 years by now, but Jesus Christ, this game has the clunkiest movement control I have ever witnessed in a game that it is ridiculously annoying and made me drop the game after three LONG hours.

I am not an Assassin's Creed saga player so I am unable to compare it with any of the other titles, but this one makes me NOT want to even discover those other ones unfortunately, but who knows - maybe I'll give Odyssey a try in the future.

Story wise I only made it to the middle part of the second act, so again - I cannot really be the proper judge here, but it felt  empty and emotionless, and most importantly - boring.

The only pro I can probably give it is the graphics, which truly feels amazing even though the game is over a decade old.
Game is just as good as I thought. Logging in was terrible. WDYM account suspended? On what basis? I literally just played the game for a day. and its my first game purchased on steam.

"A Ubisoft service is unavailable right now. Please try again later" I am extremely frustrated with Ubisoft. Why do I have to keep resetting my password whenever I want to play this game!!? The game refuses to launch unless I change my password and the same error appears again the next day. A good game but a pathetic Ubisoft launcher. Wasted my money on these developers for the last time.

By far the best Assassin's Creed ever made. While many still joke about it being "Assassin's Creed Bugnity," it's worth pointing out that the game nowadays runs remarkably well — fully optimized and boasting stunning visuals that make it feel like you're watching a movie rather than playing one.

One thing I especially want to highlight is the parkour system. In my opinion, it features one of the most satisfying parkour mechanics in the entire history of video games — fluid, responsive, and an absolute joy to use.

As for the story, it sits somewhere in the middle. It's nothing groundbreaking, but it does its job well enough, keeping you engaged and entertained throughout.

this game shows what ubisoft is capable of->
THE ATMOSPHERE
THE CHARACTERS
THE COMBAT
THE PARKOUR
each and everything is beyond imagination, i dont know why the game is so hated . it ran like smooth butter on my pc no bugs no lag and i really enjoyed it 
i will most probably be completing it 100% and i would recommend you all to play this game atleast once cause its hell a good and yes the dlc is also very fun :)
why i cant login to my ubisoft account i just dont play the game 2 weeks and i cant play the again

The most ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ controls in any video game I've ever played. Trying to move in this game is a buggy mess. Your character will move unpredictably and even teleport to different climbing spots, get stuck on objects, refuse to jump off objects, climb up invisible walls before teleporting to them. A real shame, because the setting and characters seemed interesting...

Apparently this is the "fixed" version too. Just avoid this game. The franchise only gets worse from here.

When you try to play, it downloads the Ubisoft Connect app, opens an overlay, doesn't detect the controllers, and has terrible performance. It's a shame I can't get a refund for this garbage. The problem isn't the game itself; the problem is Ubisoft Connect, which slows down performance, doesn't detect the controllers, and forces you to log in to play a game you've already paid for. The app feels like a virus.
This game sucks so bad. So buggy compared to current gen games.

I loved this game, it was a different kind of playing style where you could actually embrace being stealthy. Also, I really enjoyed the combat mechanics, combat was a little to get used to but in no time I really started to look forward to combat; especially when I got the sword of Eden.

PRO TIP: If you are scaling a wall make sure that you do not shot otherwise you will be stuck in a running loop where you are stuck in the air. If this happens you just need to reload last checkpoint/memory or you can fast travel to the Cafe Theatre.
Paris has some of the best parkour out of any assassins creed game but the combat is weird as hell so id give it 4 stars.

ACU was a massive game when i was a kid the day i got it was Christmas and new xbox 1, i must've played the game non stop for 2 or 3 weeks. all of my friends had the game everybody was playing, it was a tremendous amount of fun. ubisoft will never fix assassins creed until they truly understand what made the AC, brotherhood, unity , soo good the gameplay the replay ability the story the characters it was truly the full package and as i grow older i yearn for the old days the titles that could've been the recent releases just don't compare as a die hard fan i hope ubi gets it together soon or that I'm around to see another great AC RELEASE!

Hello, I've been trying to launch the game for a few days now and it asks for my Ubisoft password. I log in with my username and password and get an error when I try to log in. I can log in normally to other Ubisoft games, but this only happens with Assassin's Creed® Unity. Can you help me?
COOL game it has a nice story and nice animations

AC Unity is such an amazing game especially the parkour mechanic, the combat system can be brutal at the start but once you get the hang of it you'll be alright its not too bad just unforgiving. This is by far one of the best stories in the whole franchise and it interlinks with the other games nicely aswell. The platinum on this game isn't too bad if you play the game normally and collecting things as you go you'll be alright then follow some guides if needed to clear up the rest, you can also join discord groups for help with the multiplayer coop missions/trophies the coop within the game is still quite active. The coop in unity is alot better than black flags multiplayer in terms of support activity and easier to get trophies and the way that coop runs in unity I hope they bring into other future Ac games.

Absolutely amazing game, genuinely my favourite in the franchise. Paris is a beautiful map that has an endless possibility of events and a great environment for parkour. The story... WOOOOWWW. Yes. ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ amazing, love the characters and their dynamic, the only bad thing were the trail missions but even then they weren't that bad. While the combat was good, I do prefer the original AC combat where you have much more freedom and choice with your way of killing enemies. There are a few bugs but they're not game breaking, just a texture glitch every now and then. Overall and amazing game and would definitely recommend it.

 >:/
The launcher is awful and -10/10 would not recommend wasting your money or time to ubisoft, play something else from a different company so you don't fall into ragebait.

I made an account for the very first time, linked it to my Steam, and it tells me I don't have access. I searched for info everywhere and it turns out YOUR STUPID LAUNCHER HAS ALWAYS BEEN ABSOLUTE TRASH. This completely ruined my gaming experience. I was really hoping to dive in and experience one of the most famous franchises out there, but it failed all because of your moronic launcher, Ubisoft.

♥♥♥♥ UBISOFT

For me, the gameplay is really good but it's somewhat ruined by Ubisoft's launcher. The game has a great storyline, memorable characters, impressive graphics, and enjoyable gameplay. Despite the launcher issues, it's still a solid experience overall. I give it an 8/10

Assassin's Creed Unity is a decent game, but also one of the biggest missed opportunities in the series. Its greatest strengths are the breathtaking recreation of Paris, outstanding graphics that still look impressive in 2026, a dense and lively atmosphere, and arguably the best parkour system in the franchise. Exploring the city is genuinely fun. Unfortunately, the story is forgettable, Arno is an average protagonist, and the co-op mode is a major disappointment. Playing with friends is often frustrating due to connection issues and the mode feels poorly supported. Even in 2026, bugs, glitches, and odd animations still appear regularly. The game is also surprisingly demanding on modern hardware, causing unusually high CPU temperatures and performance issues compared to many newer games. Overall, Unity is enjoyable and worth playing, but it never fully delivers on its incredible potential.

Rating: 6/10.
One of the best AC games in terms of parkour when you get the mechanics down. I loved the story and the environment.

Great Game
Great Storyline
Not buggy at all like at lauch.
Lots of side missions are present.

DLC- Dead Kings
Good story could have been better.
good story good gameplay and satisfaing stealth

Completed 100% + DLC 100%

+170h played

Always had a soft spot for the setting and immersion of this game. but its quite frustrating at times to play cus of the clunky gameplay and frequent bugs. However the game still looks fantastic over 10+ years later.

AC Unity was released the same year as AC Rogue, and this was supposed to be some new-gen game. But ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, AC Unity was one of the most annoying games I have ever played. When the game was released as a new-gen title, it had bugs; however, even in 2026, it still has glitches that can cause your laptop to crash, along with you crashing out as well. This game's main purpose was the CO-OP, but it does not cooperate nor operate. That's some new invention. The most important point is the save file; you can't play new playthroughs unless you go through a meticulous process to create a new save, which sometimes can erase the old save, and that ♥♥♥♥ happened to me as well. The only good things about this retarded game was its mid-story and pretty good parkour. Aside from that, it was a major disappointment. If you think this game is worth playing, don't buy it; instead, watch a YouTube video and move on. There's nothing interesting apart from you getting rage baited. Overall rating: 2/10

This was the potential game. 
This game should have set the tone for what Assassin's Creed would come to be in recent gen(s).
It failed, mostly due to its shortcomings at launch. Regardless, this is a good game that will provide with you an unique experience others can't. 
It offers a lot of great welcoming features, though they all stand somewhere between horrendous and good in regards to polishment. Paris is unmatched. The story is ok.
ACUFixes is great mod too for those seeking even further technical complexity.

One of the best Assassin’s Creed games you can play. This is my second time completing the game, and every playthrough I discover new things, whether in the gameplay mechanics, mission design, or the different assassination approaches available in many missions.

The parkour system is still the best in the entire series, and the graphics are incredible even by today's standards. The only real weak point is the story, which feels a bit underwhelming compared to other entries in the franchise.

The Dead Kings DLC is a nice addition. It’s relatively short, but it offers an enjoyable story with a darker atmosphere that feels different from the main game. While I liked the DLC overall, it felt a bit too short and can be completed rather quickly. The connection to the Piece of Eden was handled well, although I wish the main story had focused more on Arno’s desire to avenge his father, as that plotline had much more potential than what we ultimately got.

As for the co-op multiplayer, it's still active and you can find people playing even now. However, there are some issues from time to time, such as delayed mission synchronization, enemies spawning unexpectedly, and a few glitches during online sessions.

Overall, I would give it a 9/10. I highly recommend it, whether you're a long-time Assassin’s Creed fan or someone just getting started with the series

Very nice game, my first proper AC game (after the RPG triology)

Liked the story, the setting during french revolution and side activities. DLC was pretty nice too.

8/10

the game is okay i guess...
i won't play it on linux (aka i'll never play it again) because of their bitchass launcher

and btw, this is the only AC game i own, and i bought it because it had "Unity" in its name and i thought it was made in unity game engine
parkour is a little buggy but overall the game is great

Assassin’s Creed Unity is seriously underrated. The atmosphere of Paris is incredible — the city feels alive, dense, and immersive in a way few games can match. Walking through crowded streets or climbing across rooftops during sunset looks absolutely stunning even today.

The parkour system is easily one of the best in the series. Movement feels smooth, stylish, and satisfying, especially with the dedicated up/down mechanics. Traversing the city never gets boring and truly makes you feel like an assassin.

Despite its rough launch years ago, the game aged really well. If you enjoy exploration, stealth, and amazing parkour, Unity is definitely worth playing.
If you want to play co-op with friends, don't buy this game.The game's servers in 2026 are terrible, and you won't be able to connect with your friends.

Having played Assassin's Creed Origins and Valhalla first, jumping into Unity was a massive shift. Coming from the massive RPG titles, Unity’s traditional style and congested map layout presented a real challenge at first.However, once you get used to the change, the game truly shines. The open world is spectacular, boasting incredible historical detail and fully accessible houses that make the city feel alive. The biggest takeaway is that stealth is absolutely essential here; if you break stealth, the enemies will instantly overwhelm and shoot you down. also map is not good

both game and luncher are ♥♥♥♥ in all terms my account get suspended always I get one hour a day to play game and then see ubisoft suspend my account due to my miss slip of hand not even giving me second chance to retype my passward and all the time when I boot up it ask for passward I am never going yo buy ubisoft game ever again it ruins my all day
besides from having its imperfections. it does a great job with embodying what an assassins creed game should feel like. if stealth is your thing this game is for you

I would only get this game if you are really into the Assassin's Creed games and need another one. I'm almost certain there is no way to fix the screen tearing and it definitely ruins the whole experience just enough to make you wish they had spent a little extra time fixing it. It also is quite hard to do one or two of the assassination missions and may take you a lot of tries to get there. Prepare to die a lot if you decide to buy this game.
One of the best parkours in the series, can be some times glitchy. Story is not that good . Modern day storyline is bad . World is amazing . it's a 7/10 game for me

Too ambitious for its time and restrained by the console gen it release on. It okay on PC today, but I cant think of anyone I'd recommend this to. I'm still giving this thumbs up for the recreation of Paris.

Horrible customer support from ubisoft, horrible game launcher. Good game otherwise, it is easier to play the game by pirating due to the worst game launcher known to man. I spent 5 dollars on this game on sale and still feel cheated.

Im not going to lie, i've had this in my library for literal years and never fired it up, and only decided to do so because of the growing praise for Unity in recent years. Well, I'm gonna be honest, I'm not sure what there is to praise because this game is still essentially unplayable, at least for me. In the tiny amount of playtime i forced myself through I had no less than 3 hard crashes, dozens upon dozens of hitches with a second or more of game freeze, and basically everywhere after my initial load in location in the world half renders all the buildings AT BEST. This game is still fundamentally broken and i refuse to believe otherwise. Buyer beware, maybe you get lucky and things just work out fine for you, but this game is very much still a half baked job and you are at the mercy of how it feels for your PC, and I know mine ain't a particularly weak rig.

Update: I have managed to make the game playable through further tinkering after my initial attempts. I just needed to both edit game files, more in game settings than before, AND force the game to do some things through the nvidia control panel to get it (somewhat) stable. This review will remain negative for the time being due to me feeling it unreasonable to have to do excessive tinkering (and research online for what tinkering to try) to make the game playable out of the box.

Overall good game, pretty buggy at times, reccommended to play at 60 fps or more, under that the game is feels slow and heavy. 
Disable anti-aliasing for better perf.
i opened the game saw my father die then saw my adoptive father die got high as ♥♥♥♥ and became an assassin 10/10 game would recommend

I don't know why this game is sitting at a 75% as I currently write this review, as I've seen nothing but positive reviews for this game outside of individual technical issue, but the score is unwarranted IMO. This assassins creed is my favorite of the franchise, due in no small part to the setting, world, and activities. Napoleonic Era Paris is a treat, and I world I find worth revisiting often. The game is not what it could've been, but what we got is still pretty dang good, and quite honestly I'd take 1 Unity over 10 Shadows/Valhalla's.
i really wanted to like this game. very buggy and tedious. at times even unplayable, idk if it was just my luck with performance. disappointed

Ubisoft Connect is unfit for purpose and makes the game unplayable allot of the time as it just point blank refuses to launch. I purchased this game and I should not have to spend time searching google for workarounds to get your dogsh!t launcher to run everytime I want to play!

Combat is slow and mediocre, the stealth is decent and the parkour is good, paris is well built and immersive but gets monotonous after a while and the story dialogue and certain design choices are mediocre for example all the characters in a game set during revolutionary France having strong British accents even napoleon, and ubisoft have no excuse the Ezio trillogy had accurate accents coupled with lots of native phrases and words .
Its very nice game, world details and graphics are insane

As someone who witnessed the release of AC Unity, I can safely say that this game now goes into the category "hated on release, loved when it died". Fortunately, I never was on that hate train and gave Unity a chance years later out of curiosity. And what can I say... it's such a good AC game.

The awesome parkour system definitely supports the game from beginning to end. Although the protagonist Arno moves and climbs like a superhuman, unlike Ezio for example, you still feel that he needs speed and momentum to get from one point to another. Every parkour animation is fluid and awesome to watch, without making you too slow during exploration for the sake of realism. That's why I had such a blast climbing every building in the world.

And speaking of the world, the open world has the perfect size to let you easily explore old Paris from every angle—be it the living streets on the ground, the stunning wide view when you run on the roofs, or the very detailed and realistic interior designs when you enter a building. All of this looks very good even to this day, especially thanks to the baked lighting technique AC Unity uses.

Regarding what the open world actually has to offer, I was satisfied. It was great to have a healthy mix between monumental large buildings, small urban areas, and even some countryside. The side activities were great too, except, in my opinion, for the riddles, which were way too difficult to find. The same goes for completing the skill tree, where some of the skill points you need to get feel so randomly distributed in those specific missions.

The combat here has some highs and lows. The fighting is so simple that after a while the enemies feel more like an obstacle rather than a serious threat. But the stealth "combat", even when you just insta-kill everyone, is very great. This is because, just like with the parkour, the animations are very fluid and dynamic, which avoids any clunkiness and gives you a fair chance to use your hidden blade at the very last second.

As for the story, well, it is okay—having a rather generic plot but a more unusual ending for an Assassin's Creed game. But in my opinion, the whole franchise never really shined for having deep stories, so I didn't expect one here anyway.

All in all, as someone who loves parkour games, I would especially recommend Assassin's Creed Unity for that. And if you are worried that it is still broken like it was on release, I can say that there weren't any annoying bugs during my walkthrough. I love this game and regret ignoring AC Unity for so many years!

I love this game in every detail—from the story to the visuals, detailed, high-quality, attractive and realistic environment and even the combat system. Some of the missions are overwhelming, but I still think it's a beautiful and well-crafted experience, truly ideal for any fan of the series.

Assassin’s Creed Unity:

Is not perfect, but I still enjoyed it and would recommend it to Assassin’s Creed fans.

The biggest strength of this game is the parkour. For me, Unity has some of the best parkour in the whole Assassin’s Creed series. Moving across rooftops, climbing buildings, and using the parkour up and down system feels smooth and stylish when it works well. Paris is also a great setting. The French Revolution gives the game a strong atmosphere, and the city is packed with crowds, buildings, side activities, and detail.

The gameplay loop is fun. Stealth, combat, assassinations, co-op missions, customization, and exploring Paris all give you a lot to do. The game also looks very good, even today in some areas, and the animations are still impressive.

The weaker part is the story. It is okay, but it was not as strong or memorable as I wanted it to be. Arno is fine as a main character, but the story did not hit as hard as some of the better Assassin’s Creed games. The 100% completion is also fun but grindy, especially if you want to clear everything.

Overall, Assassin’s Creed Unity is a good game with some great ideas. It has amazing parkour, a beautiful city, fun gameplay, and a lot of content, but the story holds it back.
